title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
abstract: U-Net is a convolutional network architecture for fast and precise segmentation of biomedical images. The architecture consists of a contracting path to capture context and a symmetric expanding path that enables precise localization. Skip connections copy encoder feature maps directly to the decoder at each resolution level, combining global semantic context with fine spatial detail. The network trained end-to-end from very few images and won the ISBI cell tracking challenge 2015 by a large margin. A full-resolution segmentation of a 512×512 image takes less than a second on a modern GPU.
introduction_excerpt: There is large consent that successful training of deep networks requires many thousand annotated training samples. In this paper, we present a network and training strategy that relies on the strong use of data augmentation to use the available annotated samples more efficiently. The architecture consists of a contracting path to capture context and a symmetric expanding path that enables precise localization.
